# QuickTime File Format change log

**Article**

Changes to the QuickTime File Format.

## Overview

### 2016-09-13

#### Updated

- Updated the color atom (`'colr'`) to include information on new color standards, including DCI P3, P3 D65, and ITU-R BT.2020.

### 2015-02-14

#### Added

- A new facility for timed metadata tracks has been incorporated, see [doc://com.apple.qtff/documentation/QuickTime-File-Format/Timed_metadata_media]. Introduce `'cdsc'` track reference type, see [doc://com.apple.qtff/documentation/quicktime-file-format/Track_reference_type_atom]. Added more well-known metadata data types, see [doc://com.apple.qtff/documentation/QuickTime-File-Format/Type_indicator].

### 2014-02-11

#### Updated

- Updated the section on closed caption tracks.

### 2012-08-14

#### Updated

- Corrected the values for `kQTSampleDependency_OtherSamplesDependOnThisSample` and `kQTSampleDependency_-NoOtherSampleDependsOnThisSample`.

### 2012-08-01

#### Added

- Added [doc://com.apple.qtff/documentation/quicktime-file-format/Track_exclude_from_autoselection_atom].
- Added `clcp`, `fall`, `folw`, and `forc` to [doc://com.apple.qtff/documentation/quicktime-file-format/Track_reference_type_atom].
- Added [doc://com.apple.qtff/documentation/quicktime-file-format/Text_media_information_atom], [doc://com.apple.qtff/documentation/QuickTime-File-Format/Closed_captioning_media], and [doc://com.apple.qtff/documentation/QuickTime-File-Format/Subtitle_media].
- Added [doc://com.apple.qtff/documentation/QuickTime-File-Format/Preparing_sound_and_subtitle_alternate_groups_for_use_with_Apple_devices].

#### Updated

- In [doc://com.apple.qtff/documentation/QuickTime-File-Format/User_data_atoms], expanded the description of `'tnam'` and added `'tagc'` with related section Media Characteristic Tags.
- Explained alternate groups in [doc://com.apple.qtff/documentation/quicktime-file-format/Track_header_atom/Alternate_group].
- Corrected structure of `gmhd` and `gmin` in [doc://com.apple.qtff/documentation/quicktime-file-format/Base_media_information_atom].
- General Structure of a Sample Description in [doc://com.apple.qtff/documentation/quicktime-file-format/Sample_description_atom] includes emphasized importance of data size due to occasional terminating zeroes.
- [doc://com.apple.qtff/documentation/quicktime-file-format/Timecode_media_information_atom] now documents the reserved integer after the text size.
- “Audio track” changed to “sound track” for consistency.

#### Deprecated

- Deprecated `‘rsrc’` data reference.

### 2011-07-13

#### Added

- The sound sample description v2 format along with the definition of two new sound sample description extensions, see [doc://com.apple.qtff/documentation/quicktime-file-format/Sound_sample_description_version_2].
- New atoms for the display of track aperture in different modes, see [doc://com.apple.qtff/documentation/quicktime-file-format/Track_aperture_mode_dimensions_atom].
- New sample atoms for handling out-of-order movie samples, see [doc://com.apple.qtff/documentation/QuickTime-File-Format/Sample_atoms].
- [doc://com.apple.qtff/documentation/QuickTime-File-Format/Appendix_G_Audio_priming_handling_encoder_delay_in_AAC], which treats how to handle temporal positioning of AAC audio data explicitly.

#### Updated

- The Macintosh language codes table has been updated with current language names, see [doc://com.apple.qtff/documentation/QuickTime-File-Format/Language_code_values] and the related [doc://com.apple.qtff/documentation/quicktime-file-format/Extended_language_tag_atom] defined.

### 2010-08-03

#### Updated

- Corrected the order of fields described in the [doc://com.apple.qtff/documentation/quicktime-file-format/Metadata_handler_atom] structure.

### 2010-05-03

#### Added

- Added description of clip-based metadata and specific key/value pairs for location metadata.

### 2007-09-04

#### Added

- First public release of complete, updated *QuickTime File Format Specification* with information about atoms and atom types.
- Added licensing information and disclaimer for developers.
- A QuickTime file may now contain a file type compatibility atom. See [doc://com.apple.qtff/documentation/quicktime-file-format/File_type_compatibility_atom].
- A movie atom may now contain a movie profile atom. See [doc://com.apple.qtff/documentation/quicktime-file-format/Movie_profile_atom].
- A track atom may now contain a track profile atom. See [doc://com.apple.qtff/documentation/quicktime-file-format/Track_profile_atom].
- Video sample descriptions may now contain a pixel aspect ratio atom for non-square pixels. See [doc://com.apple.qtff/documentation/quicktime-file-format/Pixel_aspect_ratio] (`'pasp'`).
- Video sample descriptions may now also contain a color parameter atom. See [doc://com.apple.qtff/documentation/quicktime-file-format/Color_parameter_atom] (`'colr'`).
- Video sample descriptions may now a clean aperture atom. See [doc://com.apple.qtff/documentation/quicktime-file-format/Clean_aperture] (`'clap'`).
- MPEG-4 video and audio sample descriptions may now contain elementary stream descriptor atoms. See [doc://com.apple.qtff/documentation/quicktime-file-format/MPEG-4_elementary_stream_descriptor_atom] (`'esds'`) and [doc://com.apple.qtff/documentation/quicktime-file-format/MPEG-4_elementary_sound_stream_descriptor_atom] (`'esds'`).

#### Updated

- Modified introductory sections and atom descriptions; updated artwork and edited for technical accuracy.
- The sound description record has been expanded to represent variable bit-rate compression more accurately. See [doc://com.apple.qtff/documentation/QuickTime-File-Format/Sound_sample_descriptions].
- The section describing MPEG-4 audio has been modified. See MPEG-4 Audio in [doc://com.apple.qtff/documentation/QuickTime-File-Format/Sound_sample_data].
- It is now recommended that the file creation and modification times be set using UTC, rather than local time zones. See [doc://com.apple.qtff/documentation/QuickTime-File-Format/Calendar_date_and_time_values].
- User data text may now be encoded using either Macintosh text encoding or ISO text encoding (Unicode). See User Data Text Strings and Language Codes in [doc://com.apple.qtff/documentation/QuickTime-File-Format/User_data_atoms].
- It is now possible to specify languages using either Macintosh language codes or ISO language codes. See [doc://com.apple.qtff/documentation/QuickTime-File-Format/Language_code_values].
title: QuickTime File Format change log
